Tests the conversion of a boolean value to a string using the String constructor in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the JavaScript `String` function when it is given a boolean value. It converts the boolean value `false` to a string and asserts that the result is strictly equal to the string `"false"`. This test ensures that the `String` function correctly converts a boolean `false` to its string representation. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of type conversion in JavaScript.
